title: Bucky Brooks' 2026 NFL Mock Draft 3.0: Top Picks for Cowboys, Chiefs Revealed
body:
As the 2026 NFL Draft rapidly approaches, teams are starting to make their intentions known, and one of the most intriguing developments has been the mock draft predictions from renowned NFL analyst Bucky Brooks. In his latest mock draft, Brooks foresees a significant curveball in the top picks, with the Dallas Cowboys emerging as surprise suitors for highly touted edge rusher Rueben Bain Jr.

Bain Jr. is widely regarded as one of the top defensive prospects in the draft, boasting an impressive combination of speed, agility, and raw power. His ability to disrupt opposing offenses and create chaos in the backfield has teams salivating at the prospect of adding him to their roster. The Cowboys, in particular, are in dire need of a game-changing edge presence, having struggled to find consistency in their pass rush over the past few seasons. By selecting Bain Jr. in the first round, they would be addressing a glaring weakness and potentially catapulting their defense into the top tier of the league.

While the Cowboys' move to snag Bain Jr. is the most eye-catching development in Brooks' mock, it's not the only notable pick. The Kansas City Chiefs, Super Bowl champions just last season, have been looking to bolster their receiving corps and provide quarterback Patrick Mahomes with an additional target to exploit defenses. According to Brooks, the Chiefs will use their first-round pick to select explosive tight end Isaiah Thompson, who brings a unique combination of size, speed, and athleticism to the table. Thompson's ability to stretch the field and create mismatches would be a valuable asset for Mahomes and the Chiefs' high-powered offense.

Thompson's selection also speaks to the changing landscape of the tight end position in the NFL. Gone are the days of the traditional, blocking-oriented tight end; today's teams are looking for versatile players who can contribute in both the passing and run games. With Thompson's skills and athleticism, the Chiefs would be well-positioned to take advantage of the growing trend and create matchup problems for opposing defenses.

As the draft approaches, teams are beginning to make their priorities clear, and the moves outlined in Brooks' mock are a testament to the evolving nature of the NFL. With the Cowboys and Chiefs poised to make significant additions to their rosters, fans can expect a thrilling draft weekend filled with surprises and upsets. As the pieces begin to fall into place, one thing is certain: the 2026 NFL Draft will be an event for the ages, with far-reaching implications for the teams and players involved.

As we look ahead to the draft, it's clear that the stakes are high, and teams are willing to take calculated risks to build their rosters. With the likes of Bain Jr. and Thompson on the board, it's an exciting time to be an NFL fan, and the coming weeks will undoubtedly bring plenty of twists and turns as teams make their final preparations for the big day.
